title: EoE Crucifixes
wikitext:
[]: EoE Crucifixes             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sat Sep 16, 2006 1:11 pm I was just wondering if there has been any discussion or any theories as to what the "crucifixes of light" during Third Impact and EVA-01s departure. I initially thought they were human souls, but i realised that they couldn't be, since those were the red orbs. Maybe they were the souls of angels that hadn't appeared yet, but I was pretty much turned off by that idea. What we do know: -Occured at the same time all life is tanged -Ascends with EVA-01* after Lilith and MP Evas descend -Disappear when EVA-01 is floating in space (probably for artistic licence) *And for that matter, what exactly happened to the LnY? From what I could tell, it looked elongated. Maybe they were turning two into one? Was it just creating it's own "security device" as the CI called it? Discuss.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Sep 16, 2006 2:05 pm I belive The Eva Monkey was a little patronisingly laughed at for suggesting that the crosses were the result of as yet unawaked angels being complemented. It wasn't a terribly bad guess considering that cross shaped explosions do follow the deaths of one or two angels. I think he got it right in suggesting at the very least that they did signify something "local" at that spot. My guess is the crosses result from areas where population density is high, i.e. lots of people/things are getting tanged at that spot, and the result of the higher density is a big cross shaped explosion, a la the death of an angel or a big angelic beam ray explosion. As to why they float away in the send, I for one have no idea. Another guess might be that they are carrying the souls of people who didn't bother "reintergrating", but that's wild and rampant speculation. Perhaps they're simply taking away with them, like Yui, "something" that will prevent another third impact, some substance or energy if you will? Without an explicit reference, about the most we can say is they make a pretty picture.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sat Sep 16, 2006 2:14 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: My guess is the crosses result from areas where population density is high, i.e. lots of people/things are getting tanged at that spot, and the result of the higher density is a big cross shaped explosion, a la the death of an angel or a big angelic beam ray explosion.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Sep 16, 2006 2:51 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I belive The Eva Monkey was a little patronisingly laughed at for suggesting that the crosses were the result of as yet unawaked angels being complemented. It wasn't a terribly bad guess considering that cross shaped explosions do follow the deaths of one or two angels. I think he got it right in suggesting at the very least that they did signify something "local" at that spot. My guess is the crosses result from areas where population density is high, i.e. lots of people/things are getting tanged at that spot, and the result of the higher density is a big cross shaped explosion, a la the death of an angel or a big angelic beam ray explosion.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Sep 16, 2006 3:00 pm The most plausible suggestion I've heard is that they are explicitly linked to the fabled Way/Path to God -- mentioned only once in the series, but referenced multiple times in the CI. Blader<sequence of 4 numbers> came up with some interesting stuff over at ANF; maybe I'll try to dig it up later. Probably worth taking into account the fact that the "cross of light" imagery wasn't just used for Sachiel's attacks / suicide bombing and Zeruel's similar attacks, but with the paralleled scenes of EVA-02 and EVA-01's respective 'awakenings' in EoE, as well. The ones that form during 3I and literally "launch" the souls only resemble the others superficially, though... (Is "crucifixes" the proper plural form? It sounds so... off.)

[]:             Quote Post by Rawinder » Sat Sep 16, 2006 3:15 pm I think I like Shin-seiki's theory best: Shin-seiki wrote: I agree that the crosses are somehow produced as result of the Anti-AT Field, but I thought of them as an artifact of peoples' souls being released, not as the souls themselves. When EVA-01 wields the Lance to make the Copy Lances burst (which indicates the end of the Anti AT Field), the crosses rise up and float off...not sure what that means, other than that Shinji and Asuka won't have to contend with a world with crosses of light everywhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sat Sep 16, 2006 4:36 pm Kind of like an Anti-AT Field transmitter then?

[]:             Quote Post by Rawinder » Sat Sep 16, 2006 5:28 pm That sounds like a good way of putting it. Either that, or the crosses are the AATF itself (similar to how regular ATFs manifest themselves in various ways).

[]: Re: EoE Crucifixes             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sat Sep 16, 2006 10:33 pm Szmitten wrote: I was just wondering if there has been any discussion or any theories as to what the "crucifixes of light" during Third Impact and EVA-01s departure. I initially thought they were human souls, but i realised that they couldn't be, since those were the red orbs. Maybe they were the souls of angels that hadn't appeared yet, but I was pretty much turned off by that idea. What we do know: -Occured at the same time all life is tanged -Ascends with EVA-01* after Lilith and MP Evas descend -Disappear when EVA-01 is floating in space (probably for artistic licence) *And for that matter, what exactly happened to the LnY? From what I could tell, it looked elongated. Maybe they were turning two into one? Was it just creating it's own "security device" as the CI called it? Discuss.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sun Sep 17, 2006 5:31 am The Eva Monkey wrote: ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I belive The Eva Monkey was a little patronisingly laughed at for suggesting that the crosses were the result of as yet unawaked angels being complemented. It wasn't a terribly bad guess considering that cross shaped explosions do follow the deaths of one or two angels. I think he got it right in suggesting at the very least that they did signify something "local" at that spot. My guess is the crosses result from areas where population density is high, i.e. lots of people/things are getting tanged at that spot, and the result of the higher density is a big cross shaped explosion, a la the death of an angel or a big angelic beam ray explosion.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 17, 2006 2:27 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: After all, the show does show that the dead sea scrolls aren't 100% accurate, as demonstrated by Unit 01's consumption of the S2 unit.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sun Sep 17, 2006 4:40 pm Reichu wrote: I have a rambling thought between my ears: I recall seeing those crosses pop up from the whole planet, including the oceans .

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sun Sep 17, 2006 6:05 pm Yeah, weren't Dolphins specifically included in the list of Lilim? But I'm wondering, where do we see the "confirmation" of further Angels? I don't doubt that there are others, I'm just don't remember seeing any mention of a list even in a brief flash. But then again, I've only seen the series 8 times, and haven't slowed down all of the subliminal flashes. (this is not sarcasm, this is me being serious)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 18, 2006 8:19 am cat42 wrote: Yeah, weren't Dolphins specifically included in the list of Lilim?

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Mon Sep 18, 2006 12:36 pm @Gendo+Cross Picture (I didn't want to post it again) Cross has another meaning. It also refers to the hardships of life. Just think of Passio (I'm not sure if this is the English equivalent). So as I saw that cross on Gendo's shadow, I thought that "it meant to show that he has a big-big weight on his shoulders". Imagine Gendo from behind, holding that big cross. That shadow is kinda like that I think. And this made me think on: is EoE symbolical? Or rather what it wants to say with that? Looking at things from this perspective, I thought of how people learn: through pain. And what is a cross? An executional instrument. It is equal with tremendous pain. So I thought that in this case cross means the learning process, life. And looking at that ani-gif (very good stuff, Reichu! :)) I thought that "when one burns a piece of wood, a part of it becomes smoke, and that flies up to the sky, then vanishes, but some of it goes into the trees, which then make air of it blablabla..." (it was unimportant from that point on). So this cloth put on people would be like "as we live in this life, we burn out, a part of us remains, another flies up to do... whatever it think best "(in other words, I dunno). And again, seeing that ani-gif, red stuff comes up - as I've heared somewhere on this forum (where else? lol), the soul of the humans -, and LCL stays down. ... Did this make some sense? :roll: @Reichu's second pic All I can see are smiling, naked Reis... No more... O_O This is too much for my overburdened little heart and mind... *mental crashland* Reichu wrote: Keel's line is sorta ambiguous and it's boggled me a lot. It seems to be FOR the "all life fused together" idea, but there's a lot of other stuff that's against it.

[]:             Quote Post by felineki » Mon Sep 18, 2006 2:26 pm It's no work of art, but this should help make sense of the original:

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Mon Sep 18, 2006 3:40 pm Even though I side with the high density complementation idea my personal theory about the crosses now is that they don't mean didly squat and were just put there to make the scene look cool.

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Mon Sep 18, 2006 7:54 pm since its entirely impossible to get a good look at it...theres the possibility that thats what souls look like...remember that thats the conversation where Ritsuko is jabbering about souless shells...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 18, 2006 9:51 pm master0rolando wrote: since its entirely impossible to get a good look at it...theres the possibility that thats what souls look like...remember that thats the conversation where Ritsuko is jabbering about souless shells...

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Wed Sep 20, 2006 11:58 pm Reichu wrote: cat42 wrote: Yeah, weren't Dolphins specifically included in the list of Lilim?
